name: Celtic Bank
description_short: Celtic Bank is a Utah-chartered industrial bank and top-10 SBA lender offering SBA 7(a), SBA 504, USDA B&I, equipment, construction, renewable energy, and conventional business loans from $25K to $10M across 60+ industries.
description_long:
Celtic Bank, a subsidiary of Celtic Investment Inc., is a Utah-chartered industrial bank headquartered in Salt Lake City, Utah. Founded in 2001 by Reese S. Howell Jr., Celtic Bank is FDIC-insured and has ranked among the top 10 SBA lenders in the United States every year since 2013. The bank has funded over $4 billion to more than 10,000 businesses across 60+ industries.

Celtic Bank's flagship products are SBA 7(a) loans ranging from $350,000 to $5 million, with rates as low as Prime + 2.75% and up to 90% loan-to-value. For smaller deals, Celtic Express covers $25,000 to $150,000 and Celtic Advantage handles $150,000 to $350,000 — both with streamlined processing. SBA 504 loans range from $350,000 to $5 million for real estate and major equipment purchases. Origination fees are 3% across SBA products.

Beyond SBA lending, Celtic Bank offers a broad portfolio: USDA Business & Industry loans ($500K-$10M) for rural businesses, equipment financing ($100K-$3M), construction loans ($350K-$5M), renewable energy financing ($350K-$10M), asset-based lending ($350K-$5M), supply chain financing ($50K-$1M), working capital loans ($50K-$350K), business acquisition financing ($350K-$5M), commercial real estate loans ($350K-$5M), and conventional business loans ($350K-$5M).

Celtic Bank differentiates itself through product breadth and industry expertise. With 14 distinct lending products spanning deal sizes from $25,000 to $10 million, the bank can structure financing for scenarios that single-product lenders cannot accommodate. Their USDA B&I and renewable energy programs serve niche markets underserved by most SBA lenders. The bank works with businesses across manufacturing, healthcare, hospitality, retail, professional services, and dozens of other sectors.

As a consistent top-10 SBA lender with over two decades of operating history, Celtic Bank brings institutional credibility and underwriting depth to the small and mid-market business lending space. Prospective borrowers should note that Celtic does not publicly disclose minimum credit scores, revenue requirements, or time-in-business thresholds, and specific terms depend on deal structure and borrower qualifications.
pros: Top 10 SBA lender every year since 2013 — proven consistency
SBA rates as low as Prime + 2.75%
Up to 90% LTV on SBA 7(a) loans
USDA B&I and renewable energy programs for underserved markets
Over $4B funded with 20+ years of operating history
cons: Does not disclose minimum credit score, revenue, or time-in-business requirements
SBA 7(a) starts at $350K — smaller deals go through Celtic Express/Advantage
Limited transparency on non-SBA product rates
No online self-service application publicly visible
Primarily a wholesale/broker lender — may not lend direct in all cases

contact.address: 268 S State St, Salt Lake City, UT 84111

best_for: Mid-market businesses seeking SBA 7(a), SBA 504, USDA B&I, or specialty lending (equipment, construction, renewable energy) from a top-10 ranked SBA lender.
diagnosis: Celtic Bank is best for established small-to-medium businesses with documented revenue and credit history seeking government-backed or conventional commercial financing. The primary caveat is that their $25K–$500K minimum loan amounts exclude micro-businesses, and lack of published rates, fees, or qualification criteria requires direct contact before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 268 S State St, Salt Lake City, UT 84111
meta_description: Celtic Bank review: Top 10 SBA lender since 2013. SBA 7(a), 504, USDA B&I, equipment, CRE loans $25K-$10M. $4B+ funded across 60+ industries.
